C[Splatted: To stop monkey jumps you have to play next to the opponents stone on the edge on your side of the territory. So in this game that would be P1 in reply to o1. Notice that I can not attack this stone because if I do you can atari from the other side (n1) which stops me from connecting. This same theory still apllies to the final situation on the board (ie r1 in reply to q1).

C[illluck: Just curious, has anyone felt that the star-points on the 13 by 13 are too high?
TedSarvata: I vacillate on this question.  The influence gained on the outside through a 3-3 invasion might not be worth giving up the corner.
illluck: My reasoning is that if san-san is playable in 19 by 19, then it is definitely playable in 13 by 13.
TedSarvata: for white or for black?  with komi, it seems too slow for white

illluck: For white? Don't you mean black? But I think the stability is worth the loss of influence. On 19 by 19 building a wall along the third against the fourth line is around equal for both sides, so I really see no reason to play the star-point.

TedSarvata: If san san were so good, we'd all be opening there all the time.  I don't think it's good for white because it's too slow.  White need influence more than stability, to overcome komi, methinks.  Personally, I really like 3-4 for it's balance between stability and territory

illluck: What? Why would white need to overcome komi? Anyways, I usually open on the 3-4 as well, but I will try to open with the san-san next game (to be totally honest, I really don't think the difference between the 3-3 and 3-4 matter that much at our level). And I think you meant a balance between territory and influence (instead of stability).
C[TedSarvata: G4 might be a good choice
C[TedSarvata: clumsy - better to play F3 or H3 as a partial enclosure
C[TedSarvata: very small play for this stage of the game - L2 looks much bigger, or L6 or C6 or B5 or E12
topazg: D2 would be my choice. Black can simply play C3 and connect it out now.
C[illluck: Those jumps are awkward, since if black F11 white is separated.
C[TedSarvata: very small play for this stage of game, wait for yose
C[TedSarvata: strange play, rather than the very safe and nice N7, which forces bM9 or bM10
C[illluck: I meant this for the next move...
illluck: This is certainly weird, why not N8 to force M11 (K8 take   leaves the two black stones in atari) and then M3?

C[TedSarvata: see illuck's comment on previous move - this move should be N8 which forces bM11 capture, the wM3 - all black can do is N7 and wN4 completes the escape for this white dumpling - thanks illuck!

C[MysticXG: This is an end game move, it would've been best not to play it at this point.  Better moves include B1, which will allow your group to live, or E3 in which you threaten white's stone at D3, and if white saves it, you can atk at F2 to lengthen out your territory.
C[MysticXG: White should've protected at B5, as it left a weakness there

column2: Bad Shape.  This move is in general an inefficient move, it does little to protect black's territory.  E6 would've probably been a bit better, or B5 to atk white's weakness.

column1: C[MysticXG: Okay move, but would've been better at B1, since losing such a big cluster of stones is a lot more damaging then a slight invasion.

C[Splatted: I've decided to start reviewing my games, so I'll try to make a few comments on this game.
C[Splatted: This was obviously a mistake. I think the two premature 3-3 invasions were the main reason I won.
C[Splatted: I think this is a mistake, if you push the other way first you can play D2 in sente.
C[Splatted: This was an attempt to punish C2, I'm not sure about this move, but I think it was better than following black and playing D2.
Splatted: On further thought I definitely should have played D2. I'll explain why in later moves.
Splatted: I've changed my mind again. D2 seems to result in white living in sente, while C4 takes sente for white.
C[Splatted: If white plays E2 here black can play F2 (after B4), which results in black living in the corner and cutting white into 2 groups.
Sequence starting with white: E2, B4, B5, B3, C6, F2, E1, E3, f3, e4, g2, f4, g3, d1, f1.
After this sequence black might live in the corner, or maybe play another move on the outside first. Either way, the aji is much worse for black than playing f3, (or D2 earlier)
C[Splatted: The corner is not yet alive, black needs to play e2/e3 and live in gote.
C[Splatted: J3/4 now look odd, and the corner is not yet alive.
C[Splatted: b1 would have killed black
C[Splatted: Q15 is joseki, if you look at the position later Q16, P16, and O16 look quite odd. They take no territory, and have little influence.
C[Splatted: The left side looks alot bigger.
C[Splatted: Too close I think. Maybe C or D 9 or 10.
C[Splatted: Why not E8? I don't think the cut is bad here
C[Splatted: The white stones on the 10th line are also weak. I think this move lets them off to easily.
C[Splatted: I think f7 works better here.

C[Splatted: This was an attempt to imitate Otake Hideo, who often approaches a 3-4 and then tenukis to play elsewhere. My theory at the time was that even if white makes another move locally, his position will still be worse than if he gets a shimari, but I have since come to the conclusion that that is wrong. The corner will have more aji but white also develops along the side. Regardless of whether that's correct, I think it was a mistake in this position because position because white's pincering stones support each other.

C[Splatted: I wanted to settle this this group as quickly as possible to avoid letting white get moves that would strengthen both N3 and G4, but I can't find a satisfactory continuation so I think I should have just jumped out at P5.
C[Splatted: Should have been the connection.
C[Splatted: I thought I'd got a good result here, but I didn't consider what it would look like a after white peeped.
C[Splatted: I was trying to avoid ending up with a b2 bomber, but I think I should have just connected here. The cut at P4 would then be less severe and I can consider fighting if white peeps at O4.

C[Splatted: I didn't push at O7 because I thought white was making too much territory, and I also hoped to make some use of the aji in my P3 stones. In retrospect having a 4-stone wall would have strengthened my position a lot, so I think it would have been worth more than the few extra points white made, and white's inevitable turn at O6 also the strengthens her.
C[Splatted: Definitely should have been N8
C[Splatted: I don't think I realised the wakness in my position here. If I'd captured  it would have been very good thickness.
C[Splatted: Same problem as the previous move, but now there is no obvious strengthening move. M17 etc feel overconcentrated, so maybe something like h14 would be best.
C[Splatted: If this move had been on the top I think my result would have been quite good. Black takes a large solid territory and white's moyo seems reducible.
C[Splatted: Now I realise my weakness...
C[Splatted: N18 captures instead of seki.
C[world2049: wonder if you see E1, black lower section dies.
Splatted: I didn't, thanks for pointing it out.
